sections.heading: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co-
sections.title: Information Commissioner may cooperate with EU authorities on digital service oversight
sections.summary: The Information Commissioner may provide information and assistance to EU public authorities regarding digital service providers (DSPs) within the UK or internationally, particularly when incidents are notified under regulation 12(3). This facilitates cross-border regulatory oversight of DSPs to ensure effective supervision of digital services across jurisdictions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section enables cross-border regulatory cooperation between UK and EU authorities for digital service providers, requiring DTIAs to assess how data transfers and service operations impact EU oversight mechanisms.

requirements.text: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to EU public authorities
The Information Commissioner may cooperate with EU public authorities on digital service providers
The Information Commissioner may cooperate with EU public authorities in the event of an incident notified under regulation 12(3)

sections.source_quote: 13. The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co-operate with, a public authority in the EU if the Information Commissioner considers that to do so would be in the interests of effective supervision of digital service providers (whether inside or outside the United Kingdom), including in the event of an incident notified under regulation 12(3).

sections.heading: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
sections.title: Enforcement notices for non-compliance with data protection duties
sections.summary: The Information Commissioner may issue enforcement notices to organizations (OES or RDSPs) that have failed to meet specific data protection obligations. These notices require written responses specifying reasons for the notice, the alleged failure, and corrective actions with deadlines. The notice process includes informing the organization of alleged failures, allowing representations, and providing a reasonable time to rectify issues before potential penalties. The Commissioner must consider any representations made before serving the notice.
sections.dtia_summary: Enforcement notices require organizations to address specific data protection failures with clear timelines and corrective actions, impacting how data transfers and processing are managed across borders.

requirements.text: The Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice upon an OES or RDSP if it has reasonable grounds to believe they have failed to fulfil duties under regulation 12(1) or (2)
The Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice upon an OES or RDSP if it has reasonable grounds to believe they have failed to notify an incident under regulation 12(3)

The Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice upon an OES or RDSP if it has reasonable grounds to believe they have failed to comply with notification requirements under regulation 12(5)

The Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice upon an OES or RDSP if it has reasonable grounds to believe they have failed to comply with a direction made by the Information Commissioner under regulation 12(12)
The Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice upon an OES or RDSP if it has reasonable grounds to believe they have failed to comply with requirements stipulated in regulation 14A

The Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice upon an OES or RDSP if it has reasonable grounds to believe they have failed to comply with an information notice issued under regulation 15
The Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice upon an OES or RDSP if it has reasonable grounds to believe they have failed to comply with a direction given under regulation 16(2)(c)
The Information Commissioner may serve an enforcement notice upon an OES or RDSP if it has reasonable grounds to believe they have failed to comply with requirements stipulated in regulation 16(3)
An enforcement notice must be in writing and specify reasons for serving the notice
An enforcement notice must specify the alleged failure which is the subject of the notice
An enforcement notice must specify what steps must be taken to rectify the alleged failure and the time period during which such steps must be completed
The Information Commissioner must inform an OES or RDSP of alleged failures before serving an enforcement notice
The Information Commissioner must allow an OES or RDSP to make representations before serving an enforcement notice

sections.relevance_reason: Enforcement notices for data protection failures

sections.heading: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
sections.title: Civil proceedings for enforcement actions against organizations
sections.summary: This regulation outlines the conditions under which civil proceedings can be initiated by enforcement authorities against organizations that have appealed to the First-tier Tribunal. It specifies that proceedings may commence for injunctions, specific performance of statutory duties, or other remedies where an organization has not complied with regulatory obligations. The regulation also sets a 28-day period before proceedings can begin after the last enforcement notice is served.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes procedural safeguards for enforcement actions, including time limits and remedies available to regulators when organizations contest regulatory decisions. It impacts DTIAs by defining when and how enforcement actions can be initiated across jurisdictions.

requirements.text: Civil proceedings may be commenced against the OES for injunctions to enforce regulation 17(3A)
Civil proceedings may be commenced against the OES for specific performance of statutory duties under section 45 of the Court of Session Act 1988
Civil proceedings may be commenced against the OES for any other appropriate remedy or relief
Civil proceedings may be commenced against the RDSP for injunctions to enforce regulation 17(3A)
Civil proceedings may be commenced against the RDSP for specific performance of statutory duties under section 45 of the Court of Session Act 1988
Civil proceedings may be commenced against the RDSP for any other appropriate remedy or relief
No civil proceedings may be commenced under this regulation before the end of a 28-day period beginning with the day on which the last relevant enforcement notice was served

sections.source_quote: Where paragraph (1)(a) applies, the relevant competent authority may commence civil proceedings against the OES— (a) for an injunction to enforce the duty in regulation 17(3A); (b) for specific performance of a statutory duty under section 45 of the Court of Session Act 1988; or (c) for any other appropriate remedy or relief.

sections.heading: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
sections.title: Definitions for petroleum and gas infrastructure
sections.summary: This section defines key terms related to petroleum and gas infrastructure, including 'crude oil', 'crude oil based fuel', 'petroleum production project', and 'upstream petroleum pipeline'. The definitions specify technical thresholds for throughput, storage capacity, and operational requirements for facilities involved in oil and gas production, transmission, and distribution. These definitions apply to both Great Britain and Northern Ireland.

The definitions specify technical thresholds for throughput, storage capacity, and operational requirements for facilities involved in oil and gas production, transmission, and distribution. These definitions apply to both Great Britain and Northern Ireland.

sections.heading: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
sections.title: Definitions for gas and oil industry terms
sections.summary: This section defines specific terms used in the gas and oil sectors, including 'gas processing facility', 'gas processing operation', 'oil processing facility', and 'oil processing operations'. These definitions are critical for understanding the scope of activities covered by the regulations and ensure clarity in regulatory applications within the energy sector. This section grants the Information Commissioner the authority to provide information and assistance to entities relevant to the instrument. It outlines the Commissioner's role in facilitating access to necessary details for the functioning of the regulations. The provision applies to organizations subject to the instrument's requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: This section matters for DTIAs as it establishes the Information Commissioner's power to provide information and assistance, which may impact how data is handled across borders when regulatory compliance is required.

requirements.text: distribution system operator has the meaning given by Article 2(6) of the Gas Directive
final customer has the meaning given by Article 2(27) of the Gas Directive
foreign sector of the continental shelf has the meaning given by section 90(1) of the Energy Act 2011

gas processing facility means any facility which carries out gas processing operations in relation to piped gas and is operated otherwise than by a gas transporter and is not an LNG import or export facility

gas processing operation means any of the following operations: purifying, blending, odorising or compressing gas for pipeline introduction; removing constituent gases or separating oil/water; determining quantity/quality of gas; separating/purifying/blending/compressing gas for conversion or loading; loading gas for conveyance
gas transporter has the meaning given by section 7(1) of the Gas Act 1986
interconnector licence means a licence granted under section 7ZA of the Gas Act 1986
LNG facility has the meaning given by Article 2(11) of the Gas Directive
LNG system operator has the meaning given by Article 2(12) of the Gas Directive
oil equivalent means petroleum and, for throughput assessments, 1,100 cubic meters of petroleum at 15°C and 1 atm equals one tonne
oil processing facility means any facility which carries out oil processing operations
oil processing operations means initial blending and treatment of petroleum to produce stabilised crude oil for delivery
operator means the person who has control over pipeline conveyance or the person who commissions pipeline construction
petroleum has the same meaning as in section 1 of the Petroleum Act 1998 and includes processed petroleum
petroleum production project means a project carried out by virtue of a licence granted under the Petroleum Act 1998 or Petroleum (Production) Act 1934
piped gas means gas originating from a petroleum production project and conveyed only by pipes
pipeline means a pipe or system of pipes for the conveyance of anything
production installation has the meaning given by regulation 2(1) of the Offshore Installations (Safety Case) Regulations 2005
relevant offshore installation means an offshore installation within the Petroleum Act 1998 that carries on specific activities
storage facility has the meaning given by Article 2(9) of the Gas Directive
storage system operator has the meaning given by Article 2(10) of the Gas Directive
supply has the meaning given by Article 2(7) of the Gas Directive
supply undertaking has the meaning given by Article 2(8) of the Gas Directive
terminal includes facilities for initial blending, oil processing facilities, gas processing facilities, and reception of gas
transmission has the meaning given by Article 2(3) of the Gas Directive
transmission system operator has the meaning given by Article 2(4) of the Gas Directive
upstream petroleum pipeline means a pipeline operated as part of a petroleum production project and not a carbon dioxide pipeline
The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to entities relevant to this instrument

sections.source_quote: Distribution system operator has the meaning given by Article 2(6) of the Gas Directive; final customer has the meaning given by Article 2(27) of the Gas Directive; foreign sector of the continental shelf has the meaning given by section 90(1) of the Energy Act 2011
sections.relevance_reason: irrelevant: energy sector definitions, no data protection link

sections.heading: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
sections.title: Threshold requirements for air transport essential services
sections.summary: This section defines threshold requirements for entities providing air traffic services or air carrier services in the UK. It specifies that an entity must be licensed by the Secretary of State or Civil Aviation Authority for en-route air traffic services, or must have annual terminal passenger numbers exceeding 10 million at a UK airport. For air carriers, the threshold requires handling over 30% of annual terminal passengers at a UK airport with over 10 million passengers annually, or over 10 million total annual terminal passengers across all UK airports.
sections.dtia_summary: These threshold requirements define which entities qualify as essential service providers for air transport, impacting data transfer assessments for entities handling passenger data across borders.
sections.authority: Secretary of State, Civil Aviation Authority

requirements.text: An entity must be licensed by the Secretary of State or Civil Aviation Authority to provide en-route air traffic services in the UK
An entity must have annual terminal passenger numbers greater than 10 million at a UK airport
An air carrier must handle more than 30% of annual terminal passengers at a UK airport with over 10 million passengers annually
An air carrier must handle more than 10 million total annual terminal passengers across all UK airports

sections.source_quote: For the essential service of the provision of air traffic services (as defined in the Transport Act 2000), the threshold requirement in the United Kingdom is— (a) an entity which is granted a licence by the Secretary of State or the Civil Aviation Authority to provide en-route air traffic services in the United Kingdom; or (b) an air-traffic service provider at any airport which has annual terminal passenger numbers greater than 10 million.
sections.relevance_reason: Purely aviation licensing thresholds, no data handling

sections.heading: The Information Commissioner may give information and assistance to, and otherwise co- (cont.)
sections.title: Threshold requirements for healthcare essential services
sections.summary: This section defines threshold requirements for healthcare essential services in the UK, specifying which NHS entities qualify as operators of essential services. It covers NHS Trusts in England, Local Health Boards in Wales, Common Services Agencies in Scotland, and Health and Social Care Trusts in Northern Ireland. These entities must meet specific definitions under relevant health legislation to be considered essential service providers.
sections.dtia_summary: These healthcare threshold requirements identify which entities handle sensitive health data, impacting data transfer assessments for cross-border health information flows.

requirements.text: In England, an NHS Trust as defined in section 25 of the National Health Service Act 2006
In Wales, a Local Health Board or NHS Trust as defined in the National Health Service (Wales) Act 2006
In Scotland, the Common Services Agency for the Scottish Health Service established under section 10 of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978
In Scotland, a Health Board constituted under section 2 of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978
In Scotland, a Special Health Board constituted under section 2 of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978
In Northern Ireland, Health and Social Care Trusts within the meaning of 'HSC Trust' in section 31 of the Health and Social Care (Reform) Act (Northern Ireland) 2009

sections.source_quote: For the essential service of healthcare services the threshold requirements are— (a) in England, an NHS Trust as defined in section 25 of the National Health Service Act 2006 or a Foundation trust as defined in section 30 of the National Health Service Act 2006; (b) in Wales, a Local Health Board or NHS Trust as defined in the National Health Service (Wales) Act 2: (c) in Scotland— (i) the Common Services Agency for the Scottish Health Service established under section 10 of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978; (ii) a Health Board, constituted under section 2 of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978; (iii) a Special Health Board, constituted under section 2 of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978; (d) in Northern Ireland, the Health and Social Care Trusts within the meaning of 'HSC Trust' in section 31 of the Health and Social Care (Reform) Act (Northern Ireland) 2009.
sections.relevance_reason: Defines essential service providers for health data

sections.heading: The threshold requirement which applies to the essential service of the supply of potable water
sections.title: Threshold requirement for potable water supply essential services
sections.summary: This section specifies that the threshold requirement for the essential service of potable water supply in the UK is the supply of water to 200,000 or more people. It defines which water infrastructure qualifies as an essential service provider under the NIS Regulations.
sections.dtia_summary: This threshold requirement identifies water infrastructure that handles significant populations, impacting data transfer assessments for water-related data flows.

requirements.text: The threshold requirement for potable water supply is the supply of water to 200,000 or more people

sections.source_quote: 9. The threshold requirement which applies to the essential service of the supply of potable water in the United Kingdom is the supply of water to 200,000 or more people.
